title: tetragonia verrucosa
intro: Tetragonia verrucosa, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkihi in the Maori language, is a succulent plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It is used as a vegetable and has medicinal properties due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and skin conditions, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Tetragonia verrucosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
